channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
item.description: Jack Roberts: KaneAI is an AI-powered QA testing tool that automates test generation and execution for software deployments. Users describe tests in plain English and the tool writes, runs, and validates them automatically, reportedly reducing testing costs by 50%.

item.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej demonstrates Apify MCP Connectors integrated with Hermes Agent, claiming a 10x improvement in agent power. The connector enables broader functionality through Model Context Protocol integration.

item.title: How to build reliable software with AI agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech discusses how AI coding agents can generate technical debt without proper verification. The video emphasizes that human-in-the-loop review and rigorous testing are essential to ensure agents reduce maintenance costs rather than increase output at the cost of code quality and stability.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts discusses Claude Fable 5 being unexpectedly removed and recommends running AI models locally using Ollama to maintain access independent of cloud provider actions. He demonstrates building a local NotebookLM alternative with open notebook and sets up model routing with OpenRouter across Claude, DeepSeek, and other providers.

item.title: SpaceX’s IPO Triumph, Anthropic’s Fable Fumble, OpenAI’s Price War
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss SpaceX's recent IPO success, its implications for AI competitors like OpenAI and Anthropic, Anthropic's flubbed Fable rollout, and OpenAI's potential price cuts for AI models. The conversation explores whether AI models are becoming commodities and includes commentary on Apple's Siri development.

item.title: The magic behind GenLatte: Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: GenLatte is a generative UI demo app showcased at Google Cloud Next 26 that uses Gemini to generate dynamic, personalized latte art images on demand. The app demonstrates agentic development and Agent-to-UI capabilities for real-time co-creation of user experiences on the Flutter and Firebase platforms.

item.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
item.description: Cole Medin: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding models and agents such as Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It enables workflow routing where one agent can plan a task, hand it to Claude Code for implementation, then route the output to Codex for review, with governance and cross-device session support.

item.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
item.description: AI Engineer: Double iframe sandboxing nests one iframe within another to enforce security boundaries without sacrificing functionality. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app via srcdoc into an inner frame, preventing CSP bypasses, storage collisions, and sandbox escapes while maintaining origin-specific storage isolation.

item.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology's video explores AI pair programming as a collaborative development approach, highlighting how AI tools assist developers in debugging, code review, and improving productivity. The content frames AI as an effective teammate for software development workflows.

item.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
item.description: AICodeKing: OpenRouter introduced Fusion API, a system that routes prompts to multiple models and uses a judge model to synthesize responses. The creator tested Fusion against claimed Fable-level performance and found real-world results inconsistent, with Fusion sometimes slower and more expensive than single strong models.

item.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery presents a position against fully trusting code generated by AI systems. The video discusses reasons to maintain skepticism toward autonomous code generation and advocates for developer review.

item.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
item.description: AI Revolution: Web traffic is now dominated by bot requests rather than human visits. AI systems like Google AI Overviews are answering queries before users click through to websites, and AI agents are beginning to make autonomous purchases and browse on behalf of humans. The video argues this represents a fundamental shift in how the internet functions, raising questions about whether machines are now the primary users of web infrastructure.

item.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI demonstrates how to build a custom Claude Code Agentic OS with architecture, memory, and observability components. The video covers setup in three steps, references a JARVIS system, and provides guidance on skills customization to maximize Claude Code efficiency.

item.title: Stop Prop Drilling Your React Modals (Use react-call)
item.description: Better Stack: react-call is a lightweight utility that treats React components as callable asynchronous functions, eliminating prop drilling for modals. It introduces three core architectural patterns: Root and Stack for layout isolation, Upsert for active singletons, and Mutation Flow for backend loading states.

item.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
item.description: Austin Marchese: Austin Marchese presents six specific phrases to use with Claude Code that allegedly improve development speed by 10x. He claims these techniques come from the Claude Code creator and researcher Andrej Karpathy, and cautions against one dangerous prompting pattern that wastes time and money.

item.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts describes OpenAI's Codex /goal command, which lets developers describe end states rather than babysitting step-by-step code generation. The tool enters an autonomous loop called Ralph that plans, executes, tests, and self-corrects to produce working code from high-level specifications.

item.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology presents a perspective that AI agents are transforming modern software design. The video argues that design systems and context engineering are essential for guiding intelligent agents to build consistent and correct applications using MCP.

item.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
item.description: Jack Roberts: The video outlines a workflow to build a self-improving knowledge base for Hermes by connecting an Obsidian wiki to Claude and NotebookLM, using Karpathy's LLM wiki principles. Automatic cron jobs ingest data from Granola and other sources so the agent can reference emails, meeting notes, and research without forgetting context.

item.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
item.description: AICodeKing: OpenRouter launched Fusion API, a compound model that runs prompts across multiple models in parallel and uses a judge model to synthesize results. The creators claim it matches Fable-level performance at half the cost, but benchmarking appears limited to Deep Research tasks and practical tests show inconsistent performance on coding, math, and SVG generation.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search reviews over 20 recent AI developments including language models GLM 5.2 and Kimi K2.7, video generation tools like Flex4DHuman and VideoMDM, text-to-speech system Dots, and avatar/3D projects like MeshFlow and MoVerse. The video also documents the discontinuation of Claude Fable Mythos and covers multimodal capabilities like Gemini live translation.

item.title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and frames this as a sign of emerging class stratification in AI access. The video appears to discuss implications of AI content or model restrictions.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: A YouTube video discusses a fictional scenario where the US government shut down Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after three days of public availability. The commentary raises questions about frontier AI governance, public access to powerful models, and whether safety concerns might trigger government restrictions on advanced AI systems.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: The video argues that OpenAI's market dominance has eroded as competitors like Microsoft, Apple, and Google have deployed alternative AI systems. It covers GPT-6 speculation, competitive benchmarks (Claude Fable 5 vs GPT-5.5), and OpenAI's recent strategic challenges including partnerships shifting away from GPT.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: A livestream analysis of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model, which was pulled from availability three days after launch due to a US government export-control directive. The creator benchmarks the model's performance and compares its capabilities and pricing against Claude Opus 4.8.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applies image-generation diffusion techniques to text generation using uniform state diffusion across multiple bidirectional passes. The model achieves over 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting inference from memory-bound to compute-bound, with practical benchmarks tested on code generation tasks.

item.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
item.description: Jack Roberts: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model that uses Sparse Attention to process documents 15.6x faster and at one-twentieth the compute cost of its predecessor. The model is now available on OpenRouter and filters input to focus on relevant content while ignoring irrelevant portions.

item.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
item.description: AICodeKing: GLM-5.2 is an improved coding-focused language model from Z AI with a 1 million token context window, expected open-weights release, and strong performance on coding benchmarks. It is positioned as an affordable alternative to Claude and Codex through the GLM Coding Plan subscription at approximately $8 per month.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: The US government issued an export control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers immediately. The announcement references an official communication from Anthropic regarding the access restrictions.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a novel approach and runs up to four times faster on dedicated GPUs. It features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple interfaces including a desktop app (2.0), CLI, IDE, and SDK for flexible development workflows.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler built on Angular and Phaser.js powered by Gemma 4, designed as a developer masterclass. It demonstrates vibe coding through gameplay where NPCs use an LLM to generate and execute web applications, and showcases both local model serving and cloud deployment options.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej presents a 30-minute course on Pi Agent, a framework for building real coding agents. The course covers how to master Pi Agent for AI-driven code development.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates Claude Fable 5 capabilities for website design, comparing it against Opus 4.8 across three progressive challenge levels. The video examines token efficiency, design quality details, and strategic deployment choices between model versions.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music service made and curated by musicians, designed to provide music for thinking and building activities. It is offered by Anthropic as a complementary product to its Claude AI assistant.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service made and curated by musicians, designed to accompany thinking and building work. The service provides instrumental music content optimized for focus and productivity.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent platform that automates multi-format marketing campaign creation from a single prompt, generating TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven content with consistent visual styling. The tool replaces traditional shoot-edit workflows through agentic AI systems that handle campaign ideation, visual anchor definition, and cross-format content adaptation.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Database Migration Service enables developers to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL with guidance from Gemini. The service handles schema and data type translation, such as IDENTITY to PostgreSQL equivalents, while Gemini provides explanations to help developers learn the conversion process.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: CopilotKit is a framework enabling developers to build agent-native applications with generative UI capabilities. It helps render real UI components instead of basic chat windows, supports shared state with React and Next.js apps, and enables human-in-the-loop approval workflows for AI agents.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery explores how to implement memory systems in AI agents so they can retain and recall information across interactions. The video discusses techniques for making agents maintain state and remember context from previous conversations.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against legacy enterprise software giants by building a comprehensive platform from day one rather than a single feature. She shares insights on enterprise sales strategy, the importance of customer trust and brand validation, and hiring philosophy as a competitive advantage.

item.title: Don’t miss this Claude update
item.description: Jack Roberts: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which allows launching hundreds of subagents simultaneously within a single session. The system automatically generates task plans, divides work among parallel agents, and performs verification through a separate QA crew, enabling complex multi-part operations to complete faster than sequential task execution.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: The Mixture of Experts podcast discusses Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model and its tier routing system, Apple's announced shift to NVIDIA cloud compute partnership at WWDC 2026 departing from on-device AI, and whether AI models can detect sarcasm with multimodal inputs or context alone.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing analyzes the value proposition of AI subscriptions versus API pricing, estimating that Claude Pro offers around $400 in equivalent API value for $20 monthly, while ChatGPT Plus provides roughly $700 equivalent value. Higher-tier plans like ChatGPT Pro ($200) may offer up to $14,000 in theoretical usage value if fully utilized.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic's engineering and product leaders presented updates at the Code with Claude 2026 opening keynote event in Tokyo, Japan. The keynote conveyed the company's current strategic focus and product developments.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 is facing backlash for refusing harmless prompts, invoking false safety alarms, and applying invisible guardrails that degrade performance without clear disclosure. The controversy centers on whether users can trust that they receive the full model capability or a covertly downgraded version.
